name: Atalanta
description: Atalanta (/ˌætəˈlæntə/; Ancient Greek: Ἀταλάντη, romanized: Atalántē, lit. 'equal in weight') is a heroine in Greek mythology. There are two versions of the huntress Atalanta: one from Arcadia,[2] whose parents were Iasus and Clymene[3][4] and who is primarily known from the tales of the Calydonian boar hunt and the Argonauts;[5] and the other from Boeotia, who is the daughter of King Schoeneus[6][7][5] and is primarily noted for her skill in the footrace.[6] In both versions, Atalanta was a local figure allied to the g
